Outreach > Undergraduate > Resources


Genomics/Genetics Resources for Undergraduates


These websites provide exceptional genetics and genomics information.


Informational Sites

EBI Introduction to Biology - a no-nonsense introduction to molecular biolgy with an emphasis on genomics and bioinformatics. Intended for students with an interest in science but without a background in biolgy. Written by the European Bioinformatics Institute.


NCBI Science Primer - Information about bioinformatics, genome mapping, molecular genetics molecular modeling, SNPs, ESTs, microarray technology, etc


NCBI Human Genome Resources - A guide to online information resources for human genome research.


Nature's Genome Gateway - A comprehensive web resource devoted to genomics. Access to all published papers and materials is free.


Saccharomyces Genome Database (SGD) - SGD is a scientific database of the molecular biology and genetics of the yeast Saccharomyces cerevisiae, which is commonly known as bakers or budding yeast.


Science News Presented by BIO - Links to current articles, presented by the Biotechnology Industry Organization.


NHGRI Education - Diverse educational materials about genetics and genomics from the National Human Genome Research Institute.


Access Excellence - Though geared towards educators, this website is a great resource for the most up-to-date genetic news.

Glossaries

NHGRI Talking Glossary of Genetic Terms - The National Human Genome Research Institute's glossary to help people without scientific backgrounds to understand the terms and concepts used in genetic research.


GeneEd Biotechnology Glossary - Comprehensive biotechnology glossary.

Government and Laboratory Sites

National Human Genome Research Institute (NHGRI)


National Center for Genome Resources (NCGR)


Joint Genome Institute (JGI)


Lawrence Barkeley National Laboratory (Berkeley Lab)


National Science Foundation (NSF)


Department of Energy


Lawrence Hall of Science

This page has been accessed times.
